title: Payload gimbal redesign: reject GimbalTech unit, adopt K-200 custom gimbal P-1154 Rev C
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-216.

Raised by: Cass Doyle (Payload Systems Engineer), 2024-06-25.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Felix Herrera (Systems Integration), 2024-07-02.

Summary: Reject the GimbalTech 2-axis gimbal and replace with the Meridian Dynamics 3-axis gimbal assembly (P-1154 Rev C), using GB-Systems hollow-shaft motors (P-1155 pitch, P-1156 roll, P-1157 yaw) and Meridian Dynamics SimpleBGC 32-bit extended controller (P-1158).

Rationale: The GimbalTech gimbal exhibited 8.2Hz structural resonance during integration testing (KES-137, gimbal resonance bug under KES-134 epic). The K-200 airframe wing flutter mode is 8–9Hz per DOC-407 loads analysis. At resonance the camera image jitter exceeded ±4° peak-to-peak — far outside the ±0.5° requirement in DOC-449 (Payload ICD). Active electronic stabilisation in SRC-021 (gimbal_driver.py) cannot compensate for structural resonance at the same frequency as the control bandwidth limit (Sam Dreher confirmed at WIKI-038).

The redesigned P-1154 Rev C uses a stiffer CFRP frame with a natural frequency of 24Hz, confirmed by bench test with induced vibration at 8.2Hz achieving ±0.08° jitter (vs ±4° for GimbalTech). The SimpleBGC 32-bit controller (P-1158) provides an independent onboard IMU for attitude feedback. The gimbal now covers 3 axes (pitch, roll, yaw) rather than 2 (pitch, roll), providing additional stabilisation margin in banked turns.

Impact:
- P-1154: Updated to Rev C (new mechanical design, CFRP frame)
- P-1155, P-1156, P-1157: New GB-Systems motors (previously GimbalTech motors)
- P-1158: New SimpleBGC 32-bit controller board (previously 8-bit GimbalTech board)
- DOC-449: Payload System ICD to be updated for P-1158 interface and 3-axis spec
- SRC-021 (gimbal_driver.py): Updated for P-1158 interface — Cass Doyle
- DRW-519: Gimbal Mount Detail drawing to be updated
- Qualification: Flight test per DOC-449 acceptance criteria required
